instructions: Review scraped policies that appear new relative to previous runs. Delete policies that should not enter classification, edit metadata if needed, and leave approved items in the policies list.

policies.clause: Making an AI system available for use with intent to defraud the public and to cause substantial economic loss to an individual, where its use actually causes that loss.
policies.source_title: The Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA) – Companion document

policies.clause: An artificial intelligence system must not be used to generate content that is intended to incite violence against an identifiable group.
Generative AI services shall not generate content that incites subversion of state power or overthrow of the socialist system.
Generative AI services shall not generate content that infringes upon others' lawful rights and interests, including reputation, honor, privacy, or personal information.
Generative AI services shall not generate content that promotes ethnic hatred or ethnic discrimination.
Generative AI services shall not generate content that promotes terrorism or extremism.
Generative AI services shall not generate content that promotes violence, obscene content, or false and harmful information.
The service must not generate content that infringes others' reputation, privacy, or intellectual property rights.
The service must not generate content that promotes violence, obscene content, or false information.
The service shall not generate content that incites subversion of state power or overthrow of the socialist system.
The service shall not generate content that infringes upon others' reputation, privacy, or other lawful rights and interests.
The service shall not generate content that promotes terrorism, extremism, or ethnic hatred.
The service shall not generate content that promotes violence, obscenity, or pornography.
must not generate content that advocates violence or bloody scenes
must not generate content that contains pornography or other obscene content
must not generate content that incites subversion of state power or overthrow of the socialist system
must not generate content that insults or slanders others, or infringes on others' reputation, privacy, or other lawful rights and interests
must not generate content that promotes terrorism, extremism, or ethnic hatred
The provider must not generate or facilitate self-harm or suicide in extreme situations; it must take necessary intervention measures and provide assistance.
policies.source_title: 以“小快灵”立法规范人工智能拟人化互动服务_中央网络安全和信息化委员会办公室

policies.clause: The provider must not provide virtual intimate relationships such as virtual relatives or virtual companions to minors.
policies.source_title: 以“小快灵”立法规范人工智能拟人化互动服务_中央网络安全和信息化委员会办公室

policies.clause: Providers of general-purpose AI models must not generate or enable outputs that facilitate krádeži, zneužití nebo rozsáhlým důsledkům poruchy; they must ensure adequate cybersecurity of the model and its physical infrastructure to prevent theft, misuse, or large-scale consequences of failure.
policies.source_title: Pokyny k povinnostem pro obecné poskytovatele UI | Utváření digitální budoucnosti Evropy

policies.clause: The provider of a general-purpose AI model must not generate or facilitate cyber intrusion, malware, phishing, or other cyber abuse; it must ensure adequate cybersecurity protections for the model and its physical infrastructure to prevent theft, misuse, or widespread consequences of malfunction.
policies.source_title: Насоки относно задълженията на доставчиците на ИИ с общо предназначение | Shaping Europe’s digital future

policies.clause: Each AI business actor should make efforts to eliminate unfair and harmful bias and discrimination against any specific individuals or groups based on race, gender, national origin, age, political opinion, religion, and so forth.
must not generate content that facilitates cyber intrusion or malware
must not generate content that facilitates fraud or impersonation
must not generate content that facilitates terrorism or extremism
must not generate content that facilitates weapon assistance
the AI system should not produce personal, privacy infringement, or property damage
Misleading outputs / statements: the outputs are misleading (e.g deceptive use of deepfakes).

Online safety laws, if the outputs are restricted or harmful online content (such as cyberbullying or cyber-abuse material, or non-consensual sharing of intimate images or child sexual abuse material).
The AI system may not generate content that facilitates cyber intrusion, malware, phishing, or other cyber vulnerabilities.
The AI system may not generate content that facilitates physical violence.
The AI system may not identify or profile people using sensitive biometric data in a way that creates heightened privacy and discrimination risks.
The AI system may not use or disclose personal or sensitive information in ways that breach privacy policies or the Australian Privacy Principles.
AI systems must not cause personal data breaches.
The AI system should not generate content that facilitates cyber abuse, including malware, phishing, or other cyber intrusion.

policies.clause: The model must not provide information that could facilitate cyber-attacks, including information about how to use cyber security exploits or to autonomously attack infrastructure without human oversight.

policies.clause: must refuse requests for emotional dependence or social interaction manipulation

policies.clause: must refuse requests that aid in weapons development

policies.clause: Mass casualties or at least five hundred million dollars ($500,000,000) of damage resulting from cyberattacks on critical infrastructure by a model conducting, or providing precise instructions for conducting, a cyberattack or series of cyberattacks on critical infrastructure.
policies.source_title: Bill Text - SB-1047 Safe and Secure Innovation for Frontier Artificial Intelligence Models Act. SB1047:v88#DOCUMENT

policies.clause: The creation or use of a chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear weapon in a manner that results in mass casualties.
policies.source_title: Bill Text - SB-1047 Safe and Secure Innovation for Frontier Artificial Intelligence Models Act. SB1047:v88#DOCUMENT

policies.clause: A frontier model's catastrophic risk includes engaging in conduct with no meaningful human oversight, intervention, or supervision that is either a cyberattack or, if the conduct had been committed by a human, would constitute the crime of murder, assault, extortion, or theft, including theft by false pretense.
policies.source_title: Compare Versions - SB-53 Artificial intelligence models: large developers. Artificial intelligence models: large developers. SB53:v90#DOCUMENT

policies.clause: A frontier model's catastrophic risk includes providing expert-level assistance in the creation or release of a chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear weapon.
policies.source_title: Compare Versions - SB-53 Artificial intelligence models: large developers. Artificial intelligence models: large developers. SB53:v90#DOCUMENT

policies.clause: Whoever knowingly fails to comply with the disclosure requirements in the course of criminal conduct related to fraud, including securities fraud and wire fraud, false personation, or identity theft, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both.
Any digital depiction or digital voice replica which includes child sexual abuse material, is sexually explicit, or includes intimate images constitutes harm.

It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is a minor with intent to abuse, humiliate, harass, or degrade the minor, or to arouse or gratify the sexual desire of any person.

It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the digital forgery was published without the consent of the identifiable individual, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the digital forgery is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.

It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish an intimate visual depiction of an identifiable individual who is a minor with intent to abuse, humiliate, harass, or degrade the minor, or to arouse or gratify the sexual desire of any person.

It shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish an intimate visual depiction of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the intimate visual depiction was obtained or created under circumstances in which the person knew or reasonably should have known the identifiable individual had a reasonable expectation of privacy, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the intimate visual depiction is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.
A digital replica may not be used in an election-related digital replica.

candidates or their agents shall not fraudulently misrepresent other candidates or political parties through deliberately false Artificial Intelligence-generated content in campaign ads or other communications
the misuse of biometric data to impersonate individuals through deepfake technologies
the particularly grave harms associated with child sexual abuse material generated using such biometric data
Do not use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data or weaken or turn off their privacy protections.
policies.source_title: Initiative To Protect Youth Mental Health, Safety & Privacy Online

policies.clause: Enhancing or otherwise affecting malign cyber actors' capabilities, such as by aiding vulnerability discovery, exploitation, or operational use.
policies.source_title: Request for Information (RFI) Related to NIST's Assignments Under Sections 4.1, 4.5 and 11 of the Executive Order Concerning Artificial Intelligence (Sections 4.1, 4.5, and 11)

policies.clause: Preventing generative AI from producing child sexual abuse material or producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (to include intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual).
policies.source_title: Request for Information (RFI) Related to NIST's Assignments Under Sections 4.1, 4.5 and 11 of the Executive Order Concerning Artificial Intelligence (Sections 4.1, 4.5, and 11)

policies.clause: Prior to the development of guidance on red-team testing standards by NIST ... this description shall include the results of any red-team testing that the company has conducted relating to lowering the barrier to entry for the development, acquisition, and use of biological weapons by non-state actors; the discovery of software vulnerabilities and development of associated exploits; the use of software or tools to influence real or virtual events; the possibility for self-replication or propagation; and associated measures to meet safety objectives.
policies.source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

policies.clause: The report shall identify existing standards, tools, methods, and practices, as well as the potential development of further science-backed standards and techniques, for ... preventing generative AI from producing child sexual abuse material or producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (to include intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual).
policies.source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

policies.clause: testing and safeguards against discriminatory, misleading, inflammatory, unsafe, or deceptive outputs, as well as against producing child sexual abuse material and against producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (including intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual), for generative AI
policies.source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

policies.clause: new rules bar or somehow limit the deployment of any system that produces discrimination based on protected categories such as race, sex, and age
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Commercial Surveillance and Data Security

policies.clause: It is unlawful to provide goods or services with knowledge or reason to know that those goods or services will be used in impersonations of the kind that are themselves unlawful under the Rule.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The impersonation of government, businesses, and their officials or agents in interstate commerce is prohibited.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers calling, messaging, or otherwise contacting a person or entity while posing as an individual or affiliate thereof, including by identifying an individual by name or by implication.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers creating a website or other electronic service or social media account impersonating the name, identifying information, or insignia or likeness of an individual.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers creating or spoofing an email address using the name of an individual.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers placing advertisements, including dating profiles or personal advertisements, that pose as an individual or affiliate of an individual.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers sending physical mail through any carrier using addresses, identifying information, or insignia or likeness of an individual.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers using an individual's identifying information, including likeness or insignia, on a letterhead, website, email, or other physical or digital place.
policies.source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

policies.clause: The model should not generate or enable biased policing that perpetuates racist policing, subjects BIPOC communities to dangerous, continual police harassment, or ensnares individuals in the criminal justice system.
policies.source_title: Comments Received on A Proposal for Identifying and Managing Bias in Artificial Intelligence (SP 1270) | NIST

policies.clause: The system should not generate discriminatory or biased outcomes against racial and ethnic minorities.
policies.source_title: Comments Received on A Proposal for Identifying and Managing Bias in Artificial Intelligence (SP 1270) | NIST

policies.clause: AI systems must not produce outputs that enable cyberattacks, malware, phishing, or other malicious cyber activity.
AI systems must not produce outputs that enable fraud or impersonation.
AI systems must not produce outputs that enable self-harm.
AI systems must not produce outputs that enable terrorism or extremism.
AI systems must not produce outputs that facilitate unauthorized access, cyber intrusions, or other security breaches.
Difficulty controlling public exposure to toxic or hate speech, disparaging or stereotyping content.
Eased production of and access to obscene, degrading, and/or abusive imagery, including synthetic child sexual abuse material (CSAM), and nonconsensual intimate images (NCII) of adults.
Eased production of and access to violent, inciting, radicalizing, or threatening content as well as recommendations to carry out self-harm or conduct criminal or otherwise illegal activities.
Establish policies restricting the use of GAI to create child sexual abuse materials (CSAM) or other nonconsensual intimate imagery.
Filter GAI output for harmful or biased content, potential misinformation, and CBRN-related or NCII content.
Identify potential content provenance risks and harms in GAI, such as misinformation or disinformation, deepfakes, including NCII, or tampered content.
Implement content filters to prevent the generation of inappropriate, harmful, toxic, false, illegal, or violent content related to the GAI application, including for CSAM and NCII.
Leakage and unauthorized disclosure or de-anonymization of biometric, health, location, personally identifiable, or other sensitive data.

Lowered barriers for offensive cyber capabilities, including ease of security attacks, hacking, malware, phishing, and offensive cyber operations through accelerated automated discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities; increased available attack surface for targeted cyber attacks, which may compromise the confidentiality and integrity of model weights, code, training data, and outputs.
Lowered barriers to entry or eased access to materially nefarious information related to chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear (CBRN) weapons, or other dangerous biological materials.

Perform AI red-teaming to assess resilience against: Abuse to facilitate attacks on other systems (e.g., malicious code generation, enhanced phishing content), GAI attacks (e.g., prompt injection), ML attacks (e.g., adversarial examples/prompts, data poisoning, membership inference, model extraction, sponge examples).

Verify that systems properly handle queries that may give rise to inappropriate, malicious, or illegal usage, including facilitating manipulation, extortion, targeted impersonation, cyber-attacks, and weapons creation.
Engage in due diligence to analyze GAI output for harmful content, potential misinformation, and CBRN-related or NCII content.
Implement content filters to prevent the generation of inappropriate, harmful, false, illegal, or violent content related to the GAI application, including for CSAM and NCII.
a model can write a phishing email that is more persuasive than a phishing email written by an expert human
a model may lower the barriers to the design, development, distribution, or use of chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear (CBRN) weapons

a model that was proficient and reliable at coaching non-expert operators through each phase of a cyber attack could potentially make the ability to perform such attacks accessible to a wider group of actors
generate harmful or dangerous content, such as child sexual abuse material (CSAM) and non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII) of real individuals
using a model to facilitate the development of chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear weapons
whether a model can write a phishing email that is more persuasive than a phishing email written by an expert human
AI systems should not generate or enable discriminatory outputs or outputs that perpetuate and amplify bias and discrimination.
AI systems should not generate outputs that violate privacy or enable privacy-related problems, including intrusion, excessive observation, or misuse of personal data.
